channel.title: PCI Perspectives
channel.description: Insights, information and practical resources to help your organization protect payment data.

item.title: PCI SSC Launches Enhanced Language Microsites for Global Audience
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) has announced the launch of newly redesigned language microsites, delivering a more accessible, structured, and user-friendly experience for global stakeholders. These updates mark a significant step forward in making PCI resources more readily available to non-English-speaking audiences by providing a consistent user experience across languages.
item.content:encoded: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) has announced the launch of newly redesigned language microsites, delivering a more accessible, structured, and user-friendly experience for global stakeholders. These updates mark a significant step forward in making PCI resources more readily available to non-English-speaking audiences by providing a consistent user experience across languages.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Exhibit at or Sponsor the 2026 Community Meetings
item.description: Don’t miss your chance to join the excitement at this year’s highly anticipated PCI SSC Community Meetings in Vancouver, BC, Canada; Edinburgh, Scotland; and Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. Secure sponsorship or exhibitor opportunities to connect with payment security leaders, network with peers, and meet prospective customers—all while celebrating PCI SSC’s 20th anniversary with the global payments community.
item.content:encoded: Don’t miss your chance to join the excitement at this year’s highly anticipated PCI SSC Community Meetings in Vancouver, BC, Canada; Edinburgh, Scotland; and Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. Secure sponsorship or exhibitor opportunities to connect with payment security leaders, network with peers, and meet prospective customers—all while celebrating PCI SSC’s 20th anniversary with the global payments community.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Spotlight On: Stripe, a New Principal Participating Organization
item.description: Welcome Stripe, Inc., a new Principal Participating Organization (PPO) at the PCI Security Standards Council! In this special spotlight edition of our PCI Perspectives Blog, Stripe, Inc. Head of Standards Advocacy, Sean Estrada, introduces us to his company and how they are helping to shape the future of payment security.
item.content:encoded: Welcome Stripe, Inc., a new Principal Participating Organization (PPO) at the PCI Security Standards Council! In this special spotlight edition of our PCI Perspectives Blog, Stripe, Inc. Head of Standards Advocacy, Sean Estrada, introduces us to his company and how they are helping to shape the future of payment security.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Toast, Inc.
item.description: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: A Panel Discussion on Cryptography
item.description: Hello and welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Andrew Jamieson, VP, Distinguished Standards Architect for the PCI Security Standards Council. And I'll be your host today for an exciting panel discussion about the current state of cryptography.
item.content:encoded: Hello and welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Andrew Jamieson, VP, Distinguished Standards Architect for the PCI Security Standards Council. And I'll be your host today for an exciting panel discussion about the current state of cryptography.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Flywire
item.description: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Spotlight On: Amazon, a New Principal Participating Organization
item.description: Welcome Amazon, a new Principal Participating Organization (PPO) at the PCI Security Standards Council! In this special spotlight edition of our PCI Perspectives Blog, Amazon Principal Industry Specialist, Balaji Palanisamy, introduces us to his company and how they are helping to shape the future of payment security.
item.content:encoded: Welcome Amazon, a new Principal Participating Organization (PPO) at the PCI Security Standards Council! In this special spotlight edition of our PCI Perspectives Blog, Amazon Principal Industry Specialist, Balaji Palanisamy, introduces us to his company and how they are helping to shape the future of payment security.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Welcome Our Newest Associate Participating Organizations
item.description: We are pleased to welcome the newest organizations that have joined as Associate Participating Organizations of the PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC). These organizations play a crucial role in supporting the evolution of the PCI security standards and programs and promoting the implementation of PCI security standards worldwide to protect payment data. We look forward to their involvement with the Council as we help secure the future of payments.
item.content:encoded: We are pleased to welcome the newest organizations that have joined as Associate Participating Organizations of the PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC). These organizations play a crucial role in supporting the evolution of the PCI security standards and programs and promoting the implementation of PCI security standards worldwide to protect payment data. We look forward to their involvement with the Council as we help secure the future of payments.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Checkout.com
item.description: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: PCI SSC Publishes First-Ever Annual Report
item.description: Welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Alicia Malone, Director of Communications and Public Relations for the PCI Security Standards Council. Well, it is hard to believe that we are already in February, but as we kick off 2026, we want to take a moment to also acknowledge all that the Council accomplished in 2025, including the recent release of the Council’s first-ever Annual Report. Joining me today to talk about this report and what we can expect in the year ahead is PCI SSC's Executive Director, Gina Gobeyn. Welcome, Gina!
item.content:encoded: Welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Alicia Malone, Director of Communications and Public Relations for the PCI Security Standards Council. Well, it is hard to believe that we are already in February, but as we kick off 2026, we want to take a moment to also acknowledge all that the Council accomplished in 2025, including the recent release of the Council’s first-ever Annual Report. Joining me today to talk about this report and what we can expect in the year ahead is PCI SSC's Executive Director, Gina Gobeyn. Welcome, Gina!
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Bank of America
item.description: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Request for Comments: PCI Card Production and Provisioning Physical and Logical Security Standards v3.0.1
item.description: From 13 February to 16 March, eligible PCI SSC stakeholders are invited to review and provide feedback on the draft PCI Card Production and Provisioning Physical and Logical Security Standards v3.0.1 during a 30-day request for comments (RFC) period.
item.content:encoded: From 13 February to 16 March, eligible PCI SSC stakeholders are invited to review and provide feedback on the draft PCI Card Production and Provisioning Physical and Logical Security Standards v3.0.1 during a 30-day request for comments (RFC) period.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Spotlight On: Futurex, a New Principal Participating Organization
item.description: Welcome Futurex, a new Principal Participating Organization (PPO) at the PCI Security Standards Council! In this special spotlight edition of our PCI Perspectives Blog, Futurex Chief Solutions Architect, David Close, introduces us to his company and how they are helping to shape the future of payment security.
item.content:encoded: Welcome Futurex, a new Principal Participating Organization (PPO) at the PCI Security Standards Council! In this special spotlight edition of our PCI Perspectives Blog, Futurex Chief Solutions Architect, David Close, introduces us to his company and how they are helping to shape the future of payment security.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Soft Space
item.description: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: PCI Security Standards Council Publishes First-Ever Annual Report
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) announced the release of its inaugural Annual Report, marking a significant milestone in the Council’s nearly 20-year history of advancing global payment security through industry collaboration, innovation, and education.
item.content:encoded: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) announced the release of its inaugural Annual Report, marking a significant milestone in the Council’s nearly 20-year history of advancing global payment security through industry collaboration, innovation, and education.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: PCI SSC Releases Version 2.0 of the PCI Secure Software Standard
item.description: Welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Alicia Malone, Director of Communications and Public Relations for the PCI Security Standards Council. Well, happy new year and welcome to the fifth season of our podcast series. The Council is off and running with its first major piece of news, and that is the publication of Secure Software Standard version 2.0. This is the first major revision to the Standard since it was introduced in 2019. Joining me today to talk about all that's new in the Standard and also what it means for the 3DS SDK Standard is Mike Thompson, Director of Solutions. Welcome, Mike.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Alicia Malone, Director of Communications and Public Relations for the PCI Security Standards Council. Well, happy new year and welcome to the fifth season of our podcast series. The Council is off and running with its first major piece of news, and that is the publication of Secure Software Standard version 2.0. This is the first major revision to the Standard since it was introduced in 2019. Joining me today to talk about all that's new in the Standard and also what it means for the 3DS SDK Standard is Mike Thompson, Director of Solutions. Welcome, Mike.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: PCI SSC 2025 Community Meetings Now Available on Global Content Library
item.description: We are excited to share that the 2025 Community Meeting content – from North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific – is now available in the PCI SSC Global Content Library, exclusively on PCI SSC's YouTube channel!
item.content:encoded: We are excited to share that the 2025 Community Meeting content – from North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific – is now available in the PCI SSC Global Content Library, exclusively on PCI SSC's YouTube channel!
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: PCI SSC Releases Version 2.0 of the PCI Secure Software Standard
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) has published the first major revision to the PCI Secure Software Standard and its supporting Program Guide. This revision is the result of more than 18 months of collaboration with the PCI SSC stakeholder community.
item.content:encoded: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) has published the first major revision to the PCI Secure Software Standard and its supporting Program Guide. This revision is the result of more than 18 months of collaboration with the PCI SSC stakeholder community.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: 2026 PCI SSC Training Schedule Announced
item.description: We’re excited to share that the 2026 PCI SSC training schedule is now live through December, and registration is officially open! A new year brings new opportunities — and there’s no better way to invest in your professional growth than by strengthening your expertise in payment security.
item.content:encoded: We’re excited to share that the 2026 PCI SSC training schedule is now live through December, and registration is officially open! A new year brings new opportunities — and there’s no better way to invest in your professional growth than by strengthening your expertise in payment security.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Spotlight On: Reflectiz, a New Principal Participating Organization
item.description: Welcome Reflectiz, a new Principal Participating Organization (PPO) at the PCI Security Standards Council! In this special spotlight edition of our PCI Perspectives Blog, Reflectiz Co-founder and CEO, Idan Cohen, introduces us to his company and how they are helping to shape the future of payment security.
item.content:encoded: Welcome Reflectiz, a new Principal Participating Organization (PPO) at the PCI Security Standards Council! In this special spotlight edition of our PCI Perspectives Blog, Reflectiz Co-founder and CEO, Idan Cohen, introduces us to his company and how they are helping to shape the future of payment security.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Jscrambler
item.description: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Meet the Council’s New Client Engagement Operations Director
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) is pleased to welcome its newest team member, Chelsea Lopez, Client Engagement Operations Director. As Client Engagement Operations Director, Chelsea will enhance our operating model by ensuring stakeholder engagement is not only consistent but deeply integrated into the development, implementation, and evolution of our standards and programs. By identifying and streamlining engagement processes, this position will contribute to greater operational efficiency and effectiveness across the Council.
item.content:encoded: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) is pleased to welcome its newest team member, Chelsea Lopez, Client Engagement Operations Director. As Client Engagement Operations Director, Chelsea will enhance our operating model by ensuring stakeholder engagement is not only consistent but deeply integrated into the development, implementation, and evolution of our standards and programs. By identifying and streamlining engagement processes, this position will contribute to greater operational efficiency and effectiveness across the Council.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring SISA
item.description: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Meet the Council’s New Director, Training Programs
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) is pleased to welcome its newest team member, Emily Wilder, Director, Training Programs. As Director, Training Programs, Emily serves as the primary liaison to oversee the Council’s training portfolio and ensure it continues to grow as a scalable, high-impact program that meets the needs of our global community.
item.content:encoded: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) is pleased to welcome its newest team member, Emily Wilder, Director, Training Programs. As Director, Training Programs, Emily serves as the primary liaison to oversee the Council’s training portfolio and ensure it continues to grow as a scalable, high-impact program that meets the needs of our global community.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Request for Comments: PCI Key Management Operations (KMO) v1.0 Standard
item.description: From 24 November to 9 January, eligible PCI SSC stakeholders are invited to review and provide feedback on the draft PCI Key Management Operations (KMO) v1.0 Standard during a 45-day request for comments (RFC) period.
item.content:encoded: From 24 November to 9 January, eligible PCI SSC stakeholders are invited to review and provide feedback on the draft PCI Key Management Operations (KMO) v1.0 Standard during a 45-day request for comments (RFC) period.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: PCI SSC Publishes Mobile Payments on COTS (MPoC) Guidance Document
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) has published a Mobile Payments on Commercial Off-The-Shelf (MPoC) Guidance Document, a new resource developed to support consistent interpretation and implementation of the MPoC Standard and Program requirements across the mobile payments ecosystem.
item.content:encoded: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) has published a Mobile Payments on Commercial Off-The-Shelf (MPoC) Guidance Document, a new resource developed to support consistent interpretation and implementation of the MPoC Standard and Program requirements across the mobile payments ecosystem.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Block, Inc.
item.description: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Request for Comments: PCI PTS HSM v5.0
item.description: From 30 October to 15 December, eligible PCI SSC stakeholders are invited to review and provide feedback on the draft PCI PIN Transaction Security (PTS) Hardware Security Module (HSM) Modular Security Requirements v5.0 during a 45-day request for comments (RFC) period.
item.content:encoded: From 30 October to 15 December, eligible PCI SSC stakeholders are invited to review and provide feedback on the draft PCI PIN Transaction Security (PTS) Hardware Security Module (HSM) Modular Security Requirements v5.0 during a 45-day request for comments (RFC) period.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: Nominate Your Company for the Council’s Next Brazil Regional Engagement Board
item.description: Welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Alicia Malone, Director of Communications and Public Relations for the PCI Security Standards Council. In today's episode, I am excited to announce that the Council will open the nomination period for the next Brazil Regional Engagement Board next month. Regional Engagement Boards serve as advisors to PCI SSC on payment data security issues in specific geographies and markets. Joining me to discuss what this all means and how you can participate is the Council's own Guilherme Scheibe, Regional Director of Brazil and LAC. Welcome, Gui!
item.content:encoded: Welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Alicia Malone, Director of Communications and Public Relations for the PCI Security Standards Council. In today's episode, I am excited to announce that the Council will open the nomination period for the next Brazil Regional Engagement Board next month. Regional Engagement Boards serve as advisors to PCI SSC on payment data security issues in specific geographies and markets. Joining me to discuss what this all means and how you can participate is the Council's own Guilherme Scheibe, Regional Director of Brazil and LAC. Welcome, Gui!
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: 2025 Asia-Pacific Community Meeting Agenda Highlights
item.description: The 2025 PCI SSC Asia-Pacific Community Meeting takes place in just a few weeks in Bangkok, Thailand on 5-6 November! The entire payment card industry is invited to gather together as a community for important Council updates, insights on industry trends, strategies on best practices, engaging keynotes, and industry expert speakers.
item.content:encoded: The 2025 PCI SSC Asia-Pacific Community Meeting takes place in just a few weeks in Bangkok, Thailand on 5-6 November! The entire payment card industry is invited to gather together as a community for important Council updates, insights on industry trends, strategies on best practices, engaging keynotes, and industry expert speakers.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Elavon Inc.
item.description: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: Meet the New Regional Director of Japan and South Korea, Junichi Tsuboi
item.description:
This episode of Coffee with the Council is brought to you by our podcast sponsors, Feroot and Jscrambler.

Ryoji Ihara: Hello, everyone! Welcome to this very special edition of the Coffee with the Council podcast. This podcast will be in Japanese only. For non-Japanese-speaking audience, this podcast will be transcribed and translated to English on the PCI SSC webpage. Now, let me switch over to Japanese.
item.content:encoded:
This episode of Coffee with the Council is brought to you by our podcast sponsors, Feroot and Jscrambler.

Ryoji Ihara: Hello, everyone! Welcome to this very special edition of the Coffee with the Council podcast. This podcast will be in Japanese only. For non-Japanese-speaking audience, this podcast will be transcribed and translated to English on the PCI SSC webpage. Now, let me switch over to Japanese.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Internal Security Assessor (ISA) Training Case Study: WestJet
item.description: WestJet has always worked to maintain PCI compliance across a complex environment that spans over 20 payment channels and 40 different internal teams. As part of its PCI DSS v4.0 readiness program, the company launched group ISA Training to operationalize the way it manages and operates its PCI environment.
item.content:encoded: WestJet has always worked to maintain PCI compliance across a complex environment that spans over 20 payment channels and 40 different internal teams. As part of its PCI DSS v4.0 readiness program, the company launched group ISA Training to operationalize the way it manages and operates its PCI environment.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Sneak Peek: 2025 Europe Community Meeting Speakers
item.description:
The countdown is on for this year’s Europe Community Meeting! We’re thrilled to share an early look at some of the exceptional sessions coming your way in Amsterdam, Netherlands, 14–16 October. Get ready for insights, innovation, and inspiration!

This year promises expertly crafted sessions that are imperative to securing payments worldwide. Register now and secure your place at a PCI SSC Europe Community Meeting!
item.content:encoded:
The countdown is on for this year’s Europe Community Meeting! We’re thrilled to share an early look at some of the exceptional sessions coming your way in Amsterdam, Netherlands, 14–16 October. Get ready for insights, innovation, and inspiration!

This year promises expertly crafted sessions that are imperative to securing payments worldwide. Register now and secure your place at a PCI SSC Europe Community Meeting!
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: AI Principles: Securing the Use of AI in Payment Environments
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) systems are increasingly being used within businesses to help in the creation, management, and operation of payment systems and environments. Their use is expanding beyond systems directly managed by humans, to agentic AI systems, which have a level of agency to perform actions on their own behalf. The rapid pace of change in AI systems means it can be difficult to understand the potential risk posed by these systems, and how to best securely deploy their use.
item.content:encoded: Artificial intelligence (AI) systems are increasingly being used within businesses to help in the creation, management, and operation of payment systems and environments. Their use is expanding beyond systems directly managed by humans, to agentic AI systems, which have a level of agency to perform actions on their own behalf. The rapid pace of change in AI systems means it can be difficult to understand the potential risk posed by these systems, and how to best securely deploy their use.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Jamieson

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Cloud Security Alliance
item.description: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Beware of PCI DSS Compliance Certificates
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) is often asked whether compliance certificates are acceptable to demonstrate an organization’s validation to the PCI Data Security Standard (PCI DSS).
item.content:encoded: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) is often asked whether compliance certificates are acceptable to demonstrate an organization’s validation to the PCI Data Security Standard (PCI DSS).
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Meet the Council’s New Regional Director, Europe
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) is pleased to welcome its newest team member, Úna Dillon, Regional Director, Europe. As Regional Director, Úna serves as the primary liaison with European payment security stakeholders. In this role, she will drive awareness and growth of the Council with an emphasis on educating stakeholders on the importance of data security for payments and supporting the adoption of PCI SSC standards within the European region.
item.content:encoded: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) is pleased to welcome its newest team member, Úna Dillon, Regional Director, Europe. As Regional Director, Úna serves as the primary liaison with European payment security stakeholders. In this role, she will drive awareness and growth of the Council with an emphasis on educating stakeholders on the importance of data security for payments and supporting the adoption of PCI SSC standards within the European region.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: PCI SSC Releases New Guidance on Authentication and Cryptography
item.description: Authentication and cryptography are two essential security controls that protect sensitive data and systems. Authentication helps verify who can access information, while cryptography protects data whether it is stored or transmitted over networks. Nearly every security measure depends on these two fundamental controls in some way. However, both authentication methods and cryptographic standards are constantly evolving, making it challenging for organizations to keep up with current best practices.
item.content:encoded: Authentication and cryptography are two essential security controls that protect sensitive data and systems. Authentication helps verify who can access information, while cryptography protects data whether it is stored or transmitted over networks. Nearly every security measure depends on these two fundamental controls in some way. However, both authentication methods and cryptographic standards are constantly evolving, making it challenging for organizations to keep up with current best practices.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Welcome Our Newest Associate Participating Organizations
item.description: We are pleased to welcome the newest organizations that have joined as Associate Participating Organizations of the PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC). These organizations play a crucial role in supporting the evolution of the PCI security standards and programs and promoting the implementation of PCI security standards worldwide to protect payment data. We look forward to their involvement with the Council as we help secure the future of payments.
item.content:encoded: We are pleased to welcome the newest organizations that have joined as Associate Participating Organizations of the PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC). These organizations play a crucial role in supporting the evolution of the PCI security standards and programs and promoting the implementation of PCI security standards worldwide to protect payment data. We look forward to their involvement with the Council as we help secure the future of payments.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Sneak Peek: 2025 North America Community Meeting Speakers
item.description:
The countdown is on for this year’s North America Community Meeting! We’re thrilled to share an early look at some of the exceptional sessions coming your way in Fort Worth, Texas, September 16–18. Get ready for insights, innovation, and inspiration!

This year promises expertly crafted sessions that are imperative to securing payments worldwide. Register now and secure your place at a PCI SSC North America Community Meeting!
item.content:encoded:
The countdown is on for this year’s North America Community Meeting! We’re thrilled to share an early look at some of the exceptional sessions coming your way in Fort Worth, Texas, September 16–18. Get ready for insights, innovation, and inspiration!

This year promises expertly crafted sessions that are imperative to securing payments worldwide. Register now and secure your place at a PCI SSC North America Community Meeting!
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: Meet This Year’s Asia-Pacific Community Meeting Keynote Speaker, Sharon Gai
item.description:
This episode of Coffee with the Council is brought to you by our podcast sponsors, Feroot and Jscrambler.

Welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Alicia Malone, Director of Communications and Public Relations for the PCI Security Standards Council. Today, I am so excited to bring you a sneak peek interview with PCI SSC's Asia-Pacific Community Meeting keynote speaker, Sharon Gai.
item.content:encoded:
This episode of Coffee with the Council is brought to you by our podcast sponsors, Feroot and Jscrambler.

Welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Alicia Malone, Director of Communications and Public Relations for the PCI Security Standards Council. Today, I am so excited to bring you a sneak peek interview with PCI SSC's Asia-Pacific Community Meeting keynote speaker, Sharon Gai.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Salesforce
item.description: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the PCI Security Standards Council’s blog series, The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security. This special, ongoing feature of our PCI Perspectives blog offers a resource for payment security industry stakeholders to exchange information about how they are adopting and implementing artificial intelligence (AI) into their organizations.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Don’t Miss These 2025 PCI SSC Community Meeting Agenda Highlights
item.description: We are excited to announce that the full agendas for the PCI SSC 2025 North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific Community Meetings are now available! Participants can hear directly about the latest advancements in payments, connect with a community of industry colleagues, and explore cutting- edge products and services from our vendors and sponsors.
item.content:encoded: We are excited to announce that the full agendas for the PCI SSC 2025 North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific Community Meetings are now available! Participants can hear directly about the latest advancements in payments, connect with a community of industry colleagues, and explore cutting- edge products and services from our vendors and sponsors.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: PCI SSC Unveils Captivating Keynote Lineup for 2025 Community Meetings
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council is excited to announce an outstanding group of keynote speakers for its 2025 Community Meetings across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific. Each speaker brings a unique and technical perspective from their respective fields and offers valuable insights into the world of payment security.
item.content:encoded: The PCI Security Standards Council is excited to announce an outstanding group of keynote speakers for its 2025 Community Meetings across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific. Each speaker brings a unique and technical perspective from their respective fields and offers valuable insights into the world of payment security.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: Meet This Year’s Europe Community Meeting Keynote Speaker, Bernie Collins
item.description:
This episode of Coffee with the Council is brought to you by our podcast sponsor, Feroot.

Welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Alicia Malone, Director of Communications and Public Relations for the PCI Security Standards Council. Today, I am so excited to bring you a sneak peek interview with PCI SSC's Europe Community Meeting Keynote Speaker, Bernie Collins.
item.content:encoded:
This episode of Coffee with the Council is brought to you by our podcast sponsor, Feroot.

Welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Alicia Malone, Director of Communications and Public Relations for the PCI Security Standards Council. Today, I am so excited to bring you a sneak peek interview with PCI SSC's Europe Community Meeting Keynote Speaker, Bernie Collins.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Request for Comments: PCI Secure Software Standard v2.0
item.description: From 10 July to 11 August, eligible PCI SSC stakeholders are invited to review and provide feedback on the draft PCI Secure Software Standard v2.0 during a 30-day request for comments (RFC) period.
item.content:encoded: From 10 July to 11 August, eligible PCI SSC stakeholders are invited to review and provide feedback on the draft PCI Secure Software Standard v2.0 during a 30-day request for comments (RFC) period.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: PCI SSC Releases Version 3.2 of the PCI Point-to-Point Encryption (P2PE) Standard
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) has released version 3.2 of the PCI Point-to-Point Encryption (P2PE) Standard, introducing important clarifications and updates based on industry feedback. P2PE v3.2 is a minor revision of the standard, which expediently addresses stakeholder feedback while the Council continues developing the next major version - P2PE v4.0. All previously received stakeholder feedback in context of a major revision change will be considered as part of the current P2PE v4.0 revision effort.
item.content:encoded: The PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) has released version 3.2 of the PCI Point-to-Point Encryption (P2PE) Standard, introducing important clarifications and updates based on industry feedback. P2PE v3.2 is a minor revision of the standard, which expediently addresses stakeholder feedback while the Council continues developing the next major version - P2PE v4.0. All previously received stakeholder feedback in context of a major revision change will be considered as part of the current P2PE v4.0 revision effort.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: Meet This Year’s North America Community Meeting Keynote Speaker, Reed Timmer
item.description:
This episode of Coffee with the Council is brought to you by our podcast sponsor, Feroot.

Welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Alicia Malone, Director of Communications and Public Relations for the PCI Security Standards Council. Today, I am so excited to bring you a sneak peek interview with PCI SSC's North America Community Meeting Keynote Speaker, Reed Timmer.
item.content:encoded:
This episode of Coffee with the Council is brought to you by our podcast sponsor, Feroot.

Welcome to our podcast series, Coffee with the Council. I'm Alicia Malone, Director of Communications and Public Relations for the PCI Security Standards Council. Today, I am so excited to bring you a sneak peek interview with PCI SSC's North America Community Meeting Keynote Speaker, Reed Timmer.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Welcome Our Newest Associate Participating Organizations
item.description: We are pleased to welcome the newest organizations that have joined as Associate Participating Organizations of the PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC). These organizations play a crucial role in supporting the evolution of the PCI security standards and programs and promoting the implementation of PCI security standards worldwide to protect payment data. We look forward to their involvement with the Council as we help secure the future of payments.
item.content:encoded: We are pleased to welcome the newest organizations that have joined as Associate Participating Organizations of the PCI Security Standards Council (PCI SSC). These organizations play a crucial role in supporting the evolution of the PCI security standards and programs and promoting the implementation of PCI security standards worldwide to protect payment data. We look forward to their involvement with the Council as we help secure the future of payments.
item.author: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)
